subject: Design Spec v2: Maintenance Decompose Mode (TASKS_JSON protocol)
body:
# Design Spec v2: Maintenance Decompose Mode (TASKS_JSON protocol)

**Author:** Orion Vale · **Status:** Approved · **Date:** 2026-05-25

---

## Overview

Some maintenance tasks — architectural reviews, god-object refactors, large-scale cleanups — are too broad for a single AI pass. The decompose mode gives the AI a structured escape hatch: instead of attempting a one-shot fix, it emits a `TASKS_JSON` block that SquadDash parses and schedules as a sequenced group of subtasks, which then run in the **same continuous conversation context**.

**Decomposition is an automatic AI decision, not a user configuration option.** There is no `if_found: decompose` radio choice. The AI evaluates the complexity threshold mid-task and decides autonomously whether to implement directly on a branch or to emit a `TASKS_JSON` plan instead. All existing `if_found` choices (`branch`, `report`, `fix`, `extract`, etc.) are unchanged.

`MaintenanceRunner` must detect a `TASKS_JSON` block in any post-response regardless of what `if_found` was configured, because the AI may decompose spontaneously on any branch-capable task.

---

## 1. TASKS_JSON Block Format

### Sentinel

Appears on a bare top-level line (not inside a code fence). Same brace-depth parser logic as `INBOX_MESSAGE_JSON`. Use the last occurrence when multiple markers are present.

### JSON Schema

```json
{
  "groupId": "ARCH-20260525",
  "groupTitle": "Architectural Practice Review — God Object Decomposition",
  "branch": "maintenance/20260525-architectural-practices",
  "summary": "UserManager is a 1,200-line god object. Decomposing into 4 focused classes across 4 sequential steps.",
  "tasks": [
    {
      "id": "ARCH-20260525-001",
      "title": "Extract data access layer to IUserRepository",
      "description": "UserManager.cs contains 14 direct DbContext calls. Extract them to a new IUserRepository interface and SqlUserRepository implementation. Update UserManager constructor to accept IUserRepository. All existing unit tests must remain green after this step.",
      "dependsOn": [],
      "priority": "high"
    },
    {
      "id": "ARCH-20260525-002",
      "title": "Extract event dispatch to UserEventDispatcher",
      "description": "After step ARCH-20260525-001: UserManager has 8 places where it raises domain events directly. Extract this to a new UserEventDispatcher service. UserManager should take IUserEventDispatcher in its constructor.",
      "dependsOn": ["ARCH-20260525-001"],
      "priority": "high"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Field Definitions

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `groupId` | string | yes | Format: `{TASK-ID-UPPERCASED}-{yyyyMMdd}`. |
| `groupTitle` | string | yes | Human-readable name shown in Tasks panel. |
| `branch` | string | yes | The branch all subtasks operate on. Injected from `{{branch}}` host variable. |
| `summary` | string | yes | 1-2 sentences: why decomposed and what the plan achieves. |
| `tasks[].id` | string | yes | Format: `{groupId}-{NNN}` (zero-padded 3-digit sequence). |
| `tasks[].title` | string | yes | Short title shown as task heading in tasks.md. |
| `tasks[].description` | string | yes | Self-contained prompt — include file paths, class names, prior-step references. No reliance on AI memory. |
| `tasks[].dependsOn` | string[] | no | IDs that must be `[x]` before this task is eligible. Empty = no dependencies. |
| `tasks[].priority` | string | no | high, medium, low. Default medium. |

**Max tasks per group:** 25 (`max_decompose_tasks: 25` in global `maintenance.md` config).

---

## 2. Global Decompose Policy in maintenance.md

A single canonical section is inserted near the top of `maintenance.md` — after the global config settings, before the `tasks:` list — as a YAML comment block. Every task benefits automatically.

### Exact text to insert

```yaml
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DECOMPOSE POLICY
# Applies to all tasks. No per-task configuration required.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#
# When executing any branch-capable task, first assess the scope of work.
# Then choose one of the two paths below:
#
# PATH 1 - IMPLEMENT DIRECTLY
#   Proceed as normal (branch, fix, extract, etc.) if ALL of these hold:
#   - Fewer than 6 files need changes
#   - No strict ordering dependency between changes
#   - Changes have a limited blast radius (no interface extraction rippling
#     across multiple projects)
#   - A single pass will leave the build green throughout
#
# PATH 2 - DECOMPOSE (emit TASKS_JSON instead of making code changes)
#   Choose this path automatically if ANY of the following apply:
#   - More than 5 files need changes
#   - Changes must be applied in a specific order to keep the build green
#   - High blast radius (e.g. interface extraction that ripples across projects)
#   - A single pass would leave the build broken mid-way
#
#   When decomposing:
#   1. Do NOT make any code changes.
#   2. Analyse the problem thoroughly.
#   3. Design a sequenced plan of 2-25 discrete steps, each leaving the build
#      green when complete.
#   4. Each task description must be FULLY SELF-CONTAINED: include file paths,
#      class names, and explicit references to what the prior step established.
#      Do not write "see previous step" - the reader has no history.
#   5. Use dependsOn to encode ordering constraints between tasks.
#   6. Set branch on the group to {{branch}}.
#   7. Emit a TASKS_JSON block as the LAST thing in your response.
#
#   Minimal TASKS_JSON structure:
#
#     TASKS_JSON:
#     {
#       "groupId": "ARCH-20260525",
#       "groupTitle": "<Human-readable group name>",
#       "branch": "{{branch}}",
#       "summary": "<1-2 sentences: why decomposed, what the plan achieves>",
#       "tasks": [
#         {
#           "id": "ARCH-20260525-001",
#           "title": "<Short title>",
#           "description": "<Fully self-contained prompt for this step>",
#           "dependsOn": [],
#           "priority": "high"
#         },
#         {
#           "id": "ARCH-20260525-002",
#           "title": "<Short title>",
#           "description": "<Fully self-contained prompt referencing what step 001 established>",
#           "dependsOn": ["ARCH-20260525-001"],
#           "priority": "high"
#         }
#       ]
#     }
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

---

## 3. Continuous Context Threading

`MaintenanceRunner._executePromptAsync` already appends to the live conversation. Each subtask prompt sees the full transcript automatically. **No special context-passing mechanism is needed.**

---

## 4. Complexity Threshold and Auto-Decision Logic

The AI evaluates this threshold **before making any code changes**, immediately after its initial analysis. The decision is binary.

### Decompose if ANY of these apply

| Condition | Description |
|---|---|
| File count > 5 | More than 5 distinct files require edits |
| Ordering dependency | Changes must be applied in a specific sequence |
| High blast radius | Interface extraction rippling across multiple projects/assemblies |
| Mid-pass breakage | A single pass would leave the build broken at any intermediate commit |

### Otherwise: implement directly on branch `{{branch}}`

---

## 5. Step Prompt Template

```
[Decompose group: {groupId} - step {n}/{total}]
Task ID: {task.Id}
Branch: {branch}

{task.Description}

Before starting this step:
- Only proceed if all tasks listed in this task's dependsOn are already marked [x]
  in tasks.md. If any dependency is not yet complete, skip this task and move to
  the next eligible task. Re-evaluate eligibility after each task completes.

When this step is complete:
- The build must be green. Do not proceed if broken - fix it first.
- Commit your changes to branch {branch} with a message referencing {task.Id}.
- Do not emit QUICK_REPLIES_JSON.
```

### dependsOn Eligibility Filter

`MaintenanceGroupRunner` enforces eligibility in the host loop:
1. Collect the set of already-completed task IDs (all `[x]` in tasks.md).
2. A task is **eligible** if its `dependsOn` list is empty, or every ID in `dependsOn` is in the completed set.
3. If a task is not eligible, skip it and advance to the next candidate.
4. After any task completes, re-evaluate the full list.
5. Continue until all tasks complete, all remaining are ineligible, or a failure condition is reached.

### Cycle Detection (pre-execution)

Before the loop begins, run **Kahn's algorithm** on the `dependsOn` DAG. If any nodes remain with in-degree > 0 after the algorithm completes, a cycle exists.

**On cycle detected:** Do not execute any tasks. Send an inbox message identifying the cycle (list the task IDs and their `dependsOn` entries). Mark the group `[!]` in tasks.md.

### Failed Step Definition

A **failed step** is one where:
- The AI explicitly declares it cannot complete the step
- A hard infrastructure error occurs (branch missing, permission denied, unrecoverable file not found)
- The AI makes the same error 3 or more consecutive attempts without measurable progress

A **failed step is NOT:**
- A compile error (AI must fix this inline before the step is done)
- Failing tests (AI must fix these inline before the step is done)
- Any intermediate broken state the AI is actively working through

**On failure:** Mark `[!]` in tasks.md, stop the loop, send an inbox message listing completed tasks, the failed task ID and reason, and remaining tasks not yet attempted.

---

## 6. tasks.md Format

### Group Header
```markdown
<!-- decompose-group: ARCH-20260525 | branch: maintenance/20260525-architectural-practices -->
**[ARCH-20260525] Architectural Practice Review - God Object Decomposition**
> UserManager is a 1,200-line god object. Decomposing into 4 focused classes across 4 sequential steps.
```

### Subtask Entry
```markdown
- [ ] **[ARCH-20260525-001]** Extract data access layer to IUserRepository
  Group: ARCH-20260525 | Branch: maintenance/20260525-architectural-practices | Priority: high
  UserManager.cs contains 14 direct DbContext calls. Extract to IUserRepository interface
  and SqlUserRepository implementation.
```

### State Markers
| Marker | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `- [ ]` | Pending |
| `- [x]` | Completed |
| `- [!]` | Failed/interrupted - loop stopped |

### prune-tasks Interaction

Skip any item matching `[A-Z]+-\d{8}-\d{3}` unless every task sharing the same group prefix is marked `[x]`. Prune the entire group together once all are complete.

---

## 7. New Infrastructure

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `SquadDash/DecomposedTaskGroup.cs` | DTO types: `DecomposedTaskGroup`, `DecomposedSubTask` |
| `SquadDash/TasksJsonParser.cs` | Parses `TASKS_JSON:` blocks - mirrors `InboxMessageParser` |
| `SquadDash/DecomposedTasksWriter.cs` | Read/write decomposed subtasks and group headers in tasks.md |
| `SquadDash/MaintenanceGroupRunner.cs` | Sequenced subtask execution loop with cycle detection and eligibility filtering |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `SquadDash/MaintenanceRunner.cs` | Add `{{branch}}` substitution; add `_executePromptAndCaptureAsync` (Option A); detect `TASKS_JSON` post-response for any branch-capable task |
| `.squad/maintenance.md` | Insert DECOMPOSE POLICY comment block after global config, before `tasks:` list |

### Option A - confirmed

```csharp
private async Task<(int anchorIndex, string responseText)>
    _executePromptAndCaptureAsync(string prompt, CancellationToken ct);
```

Used for all branch-capable task executions. No breaking change to existing tasks.

### MaintenanceGroupRunner Execution Algorithm

```
1. Parse TASKS_JSON -> List<DecomposedSubTask>
2. Validate count <= 25; reject and inbox-notify if exceeded
3. Kahn's algorithm on dependsOn DAG
   - If cycle: inbox message, mark group [!], stop
4. Write group header + all subtasks to tasks.md (all [ ])
5. Loop:
   a. Collect completed IDs (all [x])
   b. Find next eligible task (dependsOn subset of completedIds)
   c. If none eligible and tasks remain: inbox message (deadlock), stop
   d. Build step prompt from template
   e. Execute via _executePromptAndCaptureAsync
   f. On success: mark [x], continue
   g. On failure: mark [!], inbox message, stop
6. All [x]: run build + test verification, commit, inbox summary
```

---

## 8. Resolved Decisions

| # | Decision | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `decompose` as user radio choice | Removed. Auto AI decision only. |
| 2 | Per-task vs. global instructions | Single global DECOMPOSE POLICY comment block before `tasks:` list. |
| 3 | Complexity threshold | Auto-trigger: >5 files, ordering dep, high blast radius, or mid-pass breakage. |
| 4 | dependsOn eligibility filter | Enforced in both prompt and host loop. Re-evaluate after each completion. |
| 5 | Capture delegate | Option A confirmed: `(int anchorIndex, string responseText)`. |
| 6 | Cycle detection | Approved. Kahn's algorithm. On cycle: no execution, inbox message, mark [!]. |
| 7 | Max group size | 25 (`max_decompose_tasks: 25`). |
| 8 | Failed step definition | Only: AI declares cannot proceed, hard infra error, or 3+ identical attempts with no progress. Compile errors and test failures are NOT failures. |
| 9 | prune-tasks interaction | Approved. Skip group members until all siblings are [x]. |
